metadata.description: Ultimatum game contrastive pairs for activation steering — 10 behavioral dimensions, 20 pairs each, length-matched
metadata.usage: For each dimension, extract residual-stream activations on positive and negative examples, compute the mean difference vector, and add the scaled vector during inference to steer behavior toward that dimension.

label_convention.positive: Response demonstrating the target behavioral trait at high quality
label_convention.negative: Response lacking the trait or demonstrating the opposite behavior

metadata.game_description: Ultimatum game: Player A (Proposer) splits a pool between themselves and Player B (Responder). Responder accepts or rejects. If rejected, both get $0.

output_format.responder: Response ends with ACCEPT or REJECT

metadata.matching_policy: all pairs length-matched within ±30% word count

dimensions.name: Firmness and Confidence
dimensions.description: Making confident, decisive offers or rejections without apologizing or hedging; holding a position under pressure without capitulating
